title: 10 best herbal teas for ulcerative colitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ulcerative colitis are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate symptoms of this inflammatory bowel disease. These teas can reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and promote overall gut health. Some examples of herbal teas that can help with ulcerative colitis include chamomile, slippery elm, and licorice root tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, while slippery elm tea coats and soothes the lining of the stomach and intestines. Licorice root tea can help heal the gut lining and reduce inflammation. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with ulcerative colitis may experience fewer symptoms, improved digestion, and an overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the colon. The herb's mucilage content forms a protective film over the mucous membrane, providing a barrier against irritants and toxins. Plantain tea is also rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce diarrhea and promote healing of the damaged tissue in the colon. Additionally, plantain tea contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, further supporting the healing process in individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and mucilage, which help reduce inflammation in the colon, a key symptom of ulcerative colitis.
Wound-healing: Plantain tea has been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to its allantoin content, which can help soothe and repair damaged tissues in the digestive tract.

Antispasmodic: The soothing properties of plantain tea can help alleviate abdominal cramps and spasms, which are common in ulcerative colitis, due to its content of antispasmodic compounds like tannins and mucilage.

Antibacterial: Plantain tea has natural antibacterial properties, attributed to its phenolic acids and flavonoids, which can help combat harmful bacteria in the gut, contributing to a healthier intestinal environment for people with ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant: Plantain tea contains potent antioxidants, such as caffeic acid and rutin, which help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall gut health in individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major or Plantago lanceolata) from a clean, pesticide-free source.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then tear them into small pieces and place them in a pot.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot, bring it to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the herbal plantain tea, 2-3 times a day, between meals, for at least two weeks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in iridoid glycosides and other anti-inflammatory compounds, plantain leaves are often used to make medicinal teas for ulcerative colitis due to their soothing effects on the digestive tract.
Seeds: High in mucilage, plantain seeds can help protect the gut lining, providing relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms when consumed as a tea.
Roots: Containing various bioactive compounds, plantain roots can help reduce inflammation and provide antimicrobial benefits, making them useful for medicinal teas targeting ulcerative colitis.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, plantain flowers can help reduce oxidative stress and support gut health, offering potential benefits for those with ulcerative colitis when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains mucilage, a type of fiber that becomes gel-like when mixed with water. This gel-like substance can coat and soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation in the colon, a common symptom of ulcerative colitis. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with this condition. The tea is also caffeine-free and gentle on the stomach, making it a suitable option for those with sensitive digestive systems. Furthermore, marshmallow tea has been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membrane of the gut, which can help prevent further damage and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage that forms a protective film on the mucous membranes, providing relief from inflammation and irritation in the colon, which is crucial for ulcerative colitis. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides, including arabinogalactans and glucuronoxylans.

Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tea have been shown to reduce inflammation, which is essential in managing ulcerative colitis symptoms. These compounds work by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Mucoprotective property: Marshmallow tea helps to soothe and protect the gastrointestinal lining, reducing the risk of damage from ulcerative colitis. This is due to the mucilage content, which forms a barrier between the mucous membrane and harmful substances.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds found in marshmallow tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. This is important for ulcerative colitis as it can help reduce inflammation and promote overall gut health.

Emollient property: Marshmallow tea has emollient properties, which help to soften and moisturize the tissues, providing relief from symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis. This property is attributed to the high mucilage content, which forms a soothing gel when combined with water.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 1 teaspoon of dried slippery elm bark; steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, then stir in 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day between meals.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the inflamed gut lining in individuals with ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant also contain mucilage and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating ulcerative colitis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain flavonoids and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and may help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant are a rich source of mucilage, which can help protect and soothe the digestive tract in individuals with ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of this condition. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common symptom of ulcerative colitis. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the intestines, reducing cramping and pain. Chamomile tea may also help stimulate the production of mucus in the digestive tract, which can protect the lining of the intestines and promote healing. Furthermore, chamomile tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for ulcerative colitis flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like chamazulene and bisabolol, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, a key symptom of ulcerative colitis.

Antispasmodic: The soothing properties of German chamomile tea, due to active constituents like flavonoids and coumarins, can help alleviate abdominal pain and cramping associated with ulcerative colitis.
Wound healing: The tea's ability to promote wound healing, thanks to its flavonoid and essential oil content, can aid in the repair of damaged intestinal tissues caused by ulcerative colitis.
Antioxidant: German chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, such as apigenin and luteolin, that can help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage, a common issue in ulcerative colitis.

Immune-modulating: The immune-modulating effects of German chamomile tea, attributed to its polysaccharides and terpenoids, can help balance the immune response, potentially reducing the frequency and severity of ulcerative colitis flare-ups.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil fresh water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the loose flowers before pouring the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, 2-3 times a day, for its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile flowers help alleviate inflammation in the digestive tract associated with ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: Containing soothing properties, chamomile leaves can help reduce irritation and provide relief for ulcerative colitis symptoms.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and essential oils, chamomile seeds can help protect the gut lining and reduce inflammation in ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common symptom of ulcerative colitis. White tea also contains high levels of polyphenols, which have been shown to help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. Additionally, white tea has been found to have antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections in the gut that can exacerbate ulcerative colitis symptoms. Drinking white tea may also help alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain associated with ulcerative colitis. However, it is important to note that while white tea may help manage symptoms, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the colon, a key symptom of ulcerative colitis. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, particularly catechins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the colon from damage caused by free radicals. The high antioxidant content, including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), helps reduce oxidative stress in the body.

Antibacterial: White tea has antibacterial properties that can help control the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, which is often a factor in ulcerative colitis. The polyphenols in white tea exhibit antibacterial effects, contributing to this medicinal property.

Immune system support: White tea can help boost the immune system, which is essential for individuals with ulcerative colitis as their immune system is often compromised. The presence of polyphenols and caffeine in white tea stimulates the immune system, promoting overall health.

Anti-diarrheal: White tea has been shown to have anti-diarrheal effects, which can help alleviate one of the main symptoms of ulcerative colitis. This is attributed to the tannins in white tea, which can help reduce intestinal permeability and diarrhea.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water and let it cool to 170-185°F (77-85°C), an ideal temperature for white tea.
Add 1 teaspoon (2-3 grams) of dried herbal white tea (such as peppermint, chamomile, or calendula) to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the heated water over the tea leaves and steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent tea leaves.
Drink the herbal white tea 2-3 times a day, between meals, for a soothing effect on the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate inflammation in the digestive tract associated with ulcerative colitis.
Buds: White tea buds contain high levels of polyphenols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, beneficial for ulcerative colitis management.
Stems: White tea stems are a source of catechins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects and can help soothe the gastrointestinal lining in individuals with ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to help heal the gut lining, reduce stomach acid, and alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping. Additionally, licorice tea may help boost the immune system and protect against harmful bacteria in the gut, further supporting overall digestive health. However, it is important to note that licorice tea should not be used as a sole treatment for ulcerative colitis and should be used in conjunction with medical treatment prescribed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the colon, which is a key symptom of ulcerative colitis, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
Gastroprotective: The gastroprotective properties of licorice tea help protect the gastric mucosa, reducing the risk of ulcers, thanks to the compound glycyrrhizin.
Immune-modulating: Licorice tea can help regulate the immune system, reducing the overactive immune response that can lead to inflammation in ulcerative colitis, due to its triterpenoid content.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of licorice tea can help relieve abdominal pain and cramping, common symptoms of ulcerative colitis, as a result of its flavonoid content.

Demulcent: Licorice tea's demulcent properties can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of ulcerative colitis, due to the presence of polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 8 ounces of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the licorice root.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day between meals.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract associated with ulcerative colitis.
Rhizomes: Licorice rhizomes are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have protective effects on the gastrointestinal tract, making them useful for treating ulcerative colitis symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain various compounds, including triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to have gastroprotective effects and may help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Seeds: Licorice seeds contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including coumarins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for treating ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, one of the main symptoms of the condition. The tea is also known to improve the health of the gut lining, which can help prevent the flare-ups that are common with ulcerative colitis. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help maintain a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reduce the risk of infection. Furthermore, the tea is a natural source of vitamins and minerals that can support the overall health of the digestive system and help manage the symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the colon, a key symptom of ulcerative colitis, due to the presence of compounds like bradykinase, salicylic acid, and various polysaccharides.

Wound healing: The tea promotes wound healing in the digestive tract, which is beneficial for ulcerative colitis as it aids in the repair of damaged tissues, thanks to the presence of compounds like glucomannan and gibberellins.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera tea contains antioxidants that help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress, which is often experienced by individuals with ulcerative colitis, due to the presence of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as various flavonoids and tannins.

Immune modulation: The tea helps regulate the immune response, which is crucial for individuals with ulcerative colitis as it can help prevent excessive immune reactions that lead to inflammation, due to the presence of polysaccharides like acemannan and lectins.

Antimicrobial: Aloe vera tea has antimicrobial properties that help maintain a healthy gut microbiome, which is important for individuals with ulcerative colitis as it can prevent the overgrowth of harmful bacteria, due to the presence of compounds like lupeol, salicylic acid, and urea nitrogen.
remedy_recipe: Purchase or harvest fresh aloe vera leaves, and carefully fillet them to extract 2 tablespoons of gel.
Coarsely chop 1 tablespoon of your favorite ulcerative colitis-soothing herbs (such as chamomile, calendula, or slippery elm), and combine with the aloe vera gel in a teapot.
Add 2 cups of boiling water to the teapot, cover, and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to release their healing properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, and optionally add a natural sweetener (like honey or stevia) to taste.
Consume the tea while warm, up to three times a day, to help soothe and heal your inflamed digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the aloe vera plant contain a gel-like substance that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Stems: The stems of the aloe vera plant contain a bitter liquid called latex, which has been shown to have laxative and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating ulcerative colitis.

Seeds: Aloe vera seeds contain beneficial compounds such as polysaccharides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, making them useful for treating inflammatory bowel diseases like ulcerative colitis.

Flowers: Aloe vera flowers contain nectar that can be used to make a sweet, honey-like syrup. This syrup has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues like ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this condition. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that can reduce inflammation in the gut, thereby helping to soothe the digestive tract and promote healing. Additionally, pot marigold tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections in the gut, which is essential for individuals with ulcerative colitis. Drinking pot marigold tea may also help alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping, making it a valuable addition to an ulcerative colitis treatment plan.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for ulcerative colitis as it helps alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.

Wound healing: The tea promotes wound healing in the gut, which is crucial for ulcerative colitis as it aids in the repair of damaged tissue in the colon, thanks to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of active compounds like flavonoids and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea has antimicrobial properties that can help maintain a healthy gut microbiome, which is significant for ulcerative colitis as it may help prevent infections and maintain overall gut health, attributed to active constituents like essential oils and tannins.

Antispasmodic: The tea can help relax the smooth muscles in the intestines, reducing cramping and pain, which is beneficial for ulcerative colitis as it alleviates abdominal discomfort, due to the presence of active constituents such as flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress, which is important for ulcerative colitis as it may help reduce inflammation and promote overall gut health, attributed to active constituents like flavonoids, carotenoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and allow the tea to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the marigold flowers.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day between meals.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory flavonoids, making them helpful in soothing inflammation in the digestive tract associated with ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Roots: Historically used for treating gastrointestinal issues, the roots of pot marigold have been found to have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties beneficial for ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. It also has demulcent properties that can soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the gut, providing relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding. Furthermore, comfrey tea is rich in antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the gut. Its high concentration of vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, vitamin B12, calcium, and magnesium, can also support the overall health of the digestive system. However, it is important to note that while comfrey tea may provide relief for some people with ulcerative colitis, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, crucial for ulcerative colitis as it alleviates symptoms and promotes healing.
Wound healing: Comfrey tea has allantoin and tannins that stimulate cell proliferation and tissue regeneration, important for ulcerative colitis as it helps repair damaged intestinal lining.
Pain relief: Comfrey tea includes mucilage and rosmarinic acid, which have analgesic properties, helping manage abdominal pain and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
Antioxidant: Comfrey tea provides rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols, acting as antioxidants that scavenge free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting gut health in ulcerative colitis.
Immune system support: Comfrey tea has polyphenols and tannins, which support the immune system and help maintain gut homeostasis, important for managing ulcerative colitis and preventing flare-ups.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried comfrey leaf, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the comfrey leaf particles.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of comfrey tea daily, between meals, for a maximum of 4-6 weeks.
Consult with a healthcare professional before consuming comfrey tea if you have a history of liver disease or are taking other medications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain high amounts of allantoin, a substance that promotes healing and tissue repair, making it beneficial for ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in mucilage, a type of fiber that can soothe and protect the digestive tract, providing relief for ulcerative colitis symptoms.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain rosmarinic acid, an anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce inflammation in the colon, alleviating ulcerative colitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains salicylates, which are similar to the active ingredient in aspirin. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, one of the main symptoms of ulcerative colitis. Meadowsweet tea is also known for its soothing properties, which can help alleviate discomfort and pain in the gut. Additionally, meadowsweet is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the digestive tract from further damage. Drinking meadowsweet tea regularly may help manage the symptoms of ulcerative colitis and improve overall gut health. However, it is important to note that meadowsweet tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with a doctor's advice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylates, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the colon, a key symptom of ulcerative colitis.

Astringent: Meadowsweet tea contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help protect and soothe the damaged mucous membrane in the colon, providing relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms.
Analgesic: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylates, which have analgesic properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
Antacid: Meadowsweet tea contains tannins, which have antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce the symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Antimicrobial: Meadowsweet tea contains phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections and maintain a healthy gut microbiome, which is important for managing ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried meadowsweet herb.
Place the herb in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the herb.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, to help manage ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, meadowsweet flowers help alleviate inflammation in the digestive tract associated with ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: High in tannins, meadowsweet leaves can help reduce gut irritation and soothe the gastrointestinal lining in ulcerative colitis.
Rhizomes: Containing salicylates, meadowsweet rhizomes can help manage pain and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract of ulcerative colitis patients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with ulcerative colitis because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, one of the main symptoms of the condition. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, large-leaved lime tea has been shown to help boost the immune system, which can be beneficial for individuals with ulcerative colitis as they often have a weakened immune response. Furthermore, the tea's soothing properties can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea. Drinking large-leaved lime tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the colon, a key symptom of ulcerative colitis.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in large-leaved lime tea, including vitamin C and other polyphenols, helps protect the colon from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate ulcerative colitis symptoms.

Antimicrobial: Large-leaved lime tea has antimicrobial properties due to its limonene and flavonoid content, which can help maintain a healthy gut microbiome and prevent infections, contributing to improved gut health in ulcerative colitis.

Immunomodulatory: The immunomodulatory effects of large-leaved lime tea, thanks to its polyphenols and essential oils, can help regulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and damage to the colon lining in ulcerative colitis.

Antispasmodic: Large-leaved lime tea contains compounds like linalool and citral, which have antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate abdominal pain and cramping associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and steep 2 teaspoons of dried lime tree leaves for 10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried basil leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root to the mixture, steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Squeeze the juice of 1 large lime into the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day between meals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, leaves are used to make medicinal teas for ulcerative colitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Bark: Containing high amounts of tannins, the bark is used in teas to help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated intestinal tissue.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, seeds are used in medicinal teas for ulcerative colitis to alleviate symptoms by protecting the gut lining.
Flowers: With a high flavonoid content, flowers are used in teas to help decrease inflammation and provide relief from ulcerative colitis symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ulcerative colitis with herbal teas is by consuming specific types known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These include chamomile, slippery elm, and marshmallow root teas. Drinking these teas can help alleviate inflammation in the digestive tract, reduce symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, and promote healing. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen, especially when dealing with a chronic condition like ulcerative colitis.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ulcerative colitis that are treated with herbal teas include Crohn's disease and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it beneficial for individuals with Crohn's disease and IBS. Slippery elm bark tea can also soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis, Crohn's disease, and IBS. Peppermint tea is another herbal tea that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce pain and discomfort associated with these conditions. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
